_note: IPL 2026 franchises. Captains and rosters sourced from Perplexity research (Nov/Dec 2025 mini-auction; see research/out/). Spoiler-safe: no current-season results.

league.name: Indian Premier League
league.format: Double group-stage (two groups of 5), each team plays 14 league matches. Top 4 advance to playoffs: Qualifier 1 (#1 v #2), Eliminator (#3 v #4), Qualifier 2, Final.
league.impactPlayerRule: Retained for 2026 — each team can substitute one player between overs 0–14 of either innings.

primary.notes: Merged Disney+ Hotstar / JioCinema entity after the 2024 Disney-Reliance merger. Mobile plans from ₹79/mo.
Also on Fubo and Sling TV. Matches start 10:00 ET / 06:00 ET for double-headers.
Stream via Sky Go or NOW TV.

teams.name: Chennai Super Kings
teams.owner: India Cements / Chennai Super Kings Cricket Ltd.
teams.identity: The Yellow Army — five-time champions, playoff fixtures almost every season, and the spiritual home of MS Dhoni.
teams.marqueeWatch: MS Dhoni — potentially his final IPL season. Finisher's craft and wicket-keeping still the emotional centre of Chepauk nights.

teams.name: Mumbai Indians
teams.identity: Record five-time winners — a squad-depth and data-first franchise, with Wankhede dew a constant chasing ally.
teams.marqueeWatch: Jasprit Bumrah — the most complete T20 bowler in the world. His death-over yorkers remain cricket's single most-watchable craft.

teams.name: Royal Challengers Bengaluru
teams.identity: Defending champions. Chinnaswamy's short boundaries make every RCB home match a six-fest.
teams.marqueeWatch: Virat Kohli — the Chinnaswamy demigod. Chasing in red, pulls to the cow-corner stand, and a crowd that won't sit down.

teams.name: Kolkata Knight Riders
teams.identity: Eden's purple-and-gold cathedral. Mystery spin has defined KKR titles across three eras.
teams.marqueeWatch: Cameron Green — IPL's most expensive overseas signing at ₹25.20 crore. Three-format genuine all-rounder, expected to bat top-4 and bowl four overs.

teams.name: Delhi Capitals
teams.identity: Formerly Delhi Daredevils. Still chasing a maiden title; the last decade has seen repeated playoff heartbreak.
teams.marqueeWatch: Kuldeep Yadav — left-arm wrist spin, the format's most distinctive middle-overs bowler.

teams.name: Gujarat Titans
teams.owner: Torrent Group (majority stake from 2025) · CVC Capital
teams.identity: Debuted 2022 and won the title that year. 132,000-seat Motera is the largest cricket ground on earth.
teams.marqueeWatch: Rashid Khan — leg-spin wizard, still the best T20 bowler on a good day. Arguably the first pick on any global T20 XI.

teams.name: Rajasthan Royals
teams.identity: The inaugural 2008 champions under Shane Warne. Renowned academy — always the franchise to find the next Indian star.
teams.marqueeWatch: Vaibhav Suryavanshi — the teenage opener whose 2025 breakout is now the second-year storyline cricket is watching.

teams.name: Sunrisers Hyderabad
teams.identity: 2016 champions. Modern era known for 250+ totals — the hardest-hitting top order in the league.
teams.marqueeWatch: Travis Head — left-handed powerplay carnage. The modern IPL opener's template.

teams.name: Lucknow Super Giants
teams.identity: Record ₹7,090-crore franchise purchase in 2021. Still chasing first trophy; Pant-led era begins now.
teams.marqueeWatch: Rishabh Pant — IPL's most expensive buy ever (₹27 crore in 2025). Fearless left-hand strokeplay behind the stumps.

teams.name: Punjab Kings
teams.homeVenue: Maharaja Yadavindra Singh International Cricket Stadium
teams.owner: Preity Zinta · Ness Wadia · Mohit Burman · Karan Paul
teams.identity: Formerly Kings XI Punjab. Moved primary home to the new Mullanpur stadium near Chandigarh. Still no title in 18 seasons — the IPL's longest wait.
teams.marqueeWatch: Shreyas Iyer — defending-champion captain from KKR, now leading Punjab's title chase. Elegant middle-order classicism.
